steps.content: The car must have the Mechanical Technical Inspection sticker before performing the Egress Test.
Verify all side body parts are mounted.
Verify all front body parts are mounted.
Verify the driver is wearing all required equipment.
Verify arm restraints are worn.
Measure helmet clearance to the line between the top of the Front Hoop and Main Hoop. Required: at least 50 mm (2 in).
Measure helmet clearance to the line between the top of the Main Hoop and the base of the Main Hoop bracing. Required: at least 50 mm (2 in).
Verify the driver can steer the wheel without interference.
Verify the lap belt is routed above the hip bones.
Verify the lap belt is routed above the thighs.
Measure shoulder harness angle relative to horizontal. Required: between 10° above and 20° below horizontal.
Verify the shoulder harness is properly tightened.
Verify anti-submarine straps are properly tightened.
Measure elapsed egress time from when the driver removes one hand from the steering wheel to when both feet are on the ground in ready-to-run condition. Required: less than 5 seconds.
Verify the kill switch is activated during exit.
The tallest approved driver must receive the tallest driver wristband.
Place the tallest-driver wristband on the correct driver; do not hand it to the team.
If a taller driver appears after Tilt-Table approval, the team loses the Tilt-Table sticker (IN.9.1b, IN.13.3.1).
With a Tilt-Table sticker, allow a maximum of 2 drivers at a time for the 5-second exit.
Additional drivers beyond the allowed 2 must go to the end of the line.
Without a Tilt-Table sticker, teams may run as many drivers as desired at once.
